uncovered_files: PLang/Runtime2/modules/http/Config.cs (0% — accessed via scope chain, not directly)

v2_findings_status.finding_3_relative_url_false_green: FIXED — tests real provider with no BaseUrl, verifies Error.Key
v2_findings_status.finding_4_plang_detection_false_green: FIXED — mock returns application/plang content type, real provider rejects
v2_findings_status.finding_5_per_step_override_false_green: FIXED — test removed, replaced with Configure_NullProperties_NotWritten which tests real behavior
v2_findings_status.finding_6_service_identity_false_green: REMOVED — signing integration test deferred (requires full signing setup)
v2_findings_status.finding_7_execute_http_error_mapping: PARTIAL — timeout tested (75% coverage on ExecuteHttpAsync), other exceptions not tested
v2_findings_status.finding_9_upload_content_resolution: FIXED — auto-detect file, auto-detect string, explicit File/Base64/Text all tested
v2_findings_status.finding_10_headers_untested: FIXED — custom headers tested via Get_CustomHeaders_AppliedToRequest
v2_findings_status.finding_12_weak_signature_assertion: FIXED — test removed, replaced with UnsignedPlangResponse test with strong assertion
v2_findings_status.finding_13_run_action_non_generic: NOT ADDRESSED — still 0% coverage, but lower priority

findings.issue: Get_JsonResponse_ParsedCorrectly (L81) — asserts result.Value is not null but doesn't verify the parsed JSON structure. If JSON parsing returned an empty object or wrong type, test would still pass.
findings.impact: Low — JSON parsing is well-tested indirectly by other tests. But a dedicated JSON test should verify the parsed value.
findings.suggestion: Cast result.Value to JsonElement or dynamic and check the 'name' property equals 'Alice'.

findings.issue: Get_500Response_ReturnsHttpError (L237) — checks StatusCode==500 but not Error.Key. Inconsistent with Get_404Response which checks both.
findings.impact: Low — 404 test covers the key check. But for consistency and to prevent wrong error type regression.

findings.issue: Get_OnStream_UsesResponseHeadersRead (L364) — test admits in comments that the callback goal doesn't exist. Only verifies request was made, not that streaming was attempted. HandleStreamingAsync has 50% coverage, SSE/Bytes/Plang streaming at 0%.
findings.impact: Medium — streaming is a complex feature. StreamLinesAsync hits 100% (likely from the error path), but SSE parsing, byte chunking, and plang signature-per-line are untested.
findings.suggestion: This is acknowledged as a limitation. Real streaming tests would need a GoalCall that resolves to an actual goal. Consider creating a test goal or a mock callback mechanism.

findings.issue: Get_BinaryResponse_ReturnsByteArray (L407) — checks type is byte[] but not that the bytes match the input PNG header bytes.
findings.impact: Low — if bytes were corrupted, type would still be byte[]. But the PNG header is only 4 bytes, easy to verify.

findings.issue: Upload_AutoDetectFile_WhenFileExists (L131) — only checks Success. Doesn't verify the file content was uploaded (vs the string 'auto.txt' being sent as text).
findings.impact: Medium — auto-detection choosing wrong content type would go undetected.
findings.suggestion: Read the request body and verify it equals 'auto content' (the file's content, not the filename).

findings.issue: Upload_ResponseParsed_AsJson (L187) — checks Properties['StatusCode']==200 but not the parsed JSON value. If response parsing failed, the value would be null but status would still be 200.
findings.impact: Low — other tests verify JSON parsing works.
findings.suggestion: Add check on result.Value to verify JSON was parsed.

findings.issue: CreateFormContentAsync (0% coverage) — multipart form upload with @file references completely untested. SignRequestAsync at 17.6%. TryExtractSignedErrorIdentity at 0%. StreamSSEAsync, StreamBytesAsync, StreamPlangAsync all at 0%.
findings.impact: These are specialized paths. Form upload and signing are the most likely to be used. SSE/Bytes/Plang streaming are niche but important for real-world HTTP usage.
findings.suggestion: Form upload: create test with dict containing '@filepath' entry. Signing: requires identity/crypto setup, may be better as integration test. Streaming: deferred (see finding #3).

summary: Major improvement from v2. DefaultHttpProvider coverage jumped from 5.7% to 88.3% line coverage. All critical and major v2 findings are resolved — tests now exercise real provider logic via MockHttpMessageHandler injection. 7 remaining findings are all minor: weak assertions (could verify parsed values more strictly), and some specialized paths at 0% (form upload, SSE streaming, plang streaming, signing). The test suite is now honest — it exercises URL resolution, response parsing (JSON/XML/text/binary/plang rejection), error handling (404/500/timeout/NoBaseUrl/ConfigLocked/UnsignedPlang), file download with existence checks, upload with auto-detection, and config scope chain. Approved with minor suggestions.
